RESPONSES TO THE FIRST AMENDED CROSS-COMPLAINT MUST BE FILED WITHIN 30 CALENDAR DAYS OF A FILED ORDER GRANTING THIS MOTION. CROSS-COMPLAINT SHALL ALLEGE SU'S FAILURE TO DISCLOSE THE CONDITION OF THE WASHING MACHINE. CALIFORNIA LAW AND POLICY LIBERALLY ALLOWS THE FILING OF AMENDED PLEADINGS. THE CLAIMS SOUGHT TO BE ADDED TO THE CROSS-COMPLAINT ARE BASED ON THE SAME CORE SET OF ALLEGED FACTS AND DISPUTES ABOUT THOSE ALLEGED FACTS THAT ARE ALREADY ALLEGED IN THE PARTIES' PLEADINGS. NEITHER MS. ALEXEEVA NOR MR. SU HAS SHOWN THAT THEY WILL SUFFER LEGAL PREJUDICE BY THE GRANTING OF THIS MOTION OR THAT THE SKYBOX PARTIES HAVE ACTED IN BAD FAITH IN FILING THIS MOTION. NO ARGUMENT HAS BEEN MADE AS TO THE PORTION OF THE MOTION SEEKING TO AMEND THE ANSWER OF THE SKYBOX PARTIES. PLAINTIFF/CROSS-DEFENDANT YING-TSUN SU IS GRANTED LEAVE TO FILE A MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT ON A 30-DAY NOTICE. ANY OPPOSITION TO SU'S MOTION SHALL BE FILED AT LEAST 14 DAYS PRIOR TO ANY SET HEARING DATE. ANY REPLY SHALL BE DUE AT LEAST 5 DAYS PRIOR TO ANY SET HEARING DATE. Judge: Curtis E.A. Karnow; Clerk: S. Kane; Not Reported. (302/CK)

Notice Of Motion And Motion Compelling Deposition Of Natasha Alexeeva; And For Monetary And Other Sanction
Off Calendar Jul-17-2024 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

HEARING RE: DEFENDANTS PAUL HWANG and SKYBOX REALTY, INC.'s Motion Compelling Deposition Of Natasha Alexeeva; And For Monetary And Other Sanction.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

I have reviewed the report and recommendation of pro tem Philip Ward, dated July 9, 2024, as well as the papers filed connection with the motion to compel. I adopt his views as modified here.

Defendants and cross-complainants Skybox Realty and Paul Hwang filed a motion for an order compelling the in-person deposition of plaintiff (and former defendant) Alexeeva, who resides in Tiburon, Marin County. The motion was joined in by plaintiff Su.

Code of Civil Procedure section 2025.250(a) provides that the deposition of a natural person shall be taken at a place that is within 75 miles of the deponent's residence.

Defendant and cross-complainant Skybox Realty and Paul Hwang have filed a motion for an order compelling the deposition of plaintiff (and former defendant) Natalia Alexeeva, together with an award of sanctions for repeated discovery abuse and bad faith tactics. Defendant Su filed a joinder.

In response to the motion, counsel for Ms. Alexeeva maintains that he has been willing to produce her for deposition all along, on condition that the deposition be taken remotely, via Zoom, as all proceedings to date have been conducted in this almost four year old lawsuit. Alexeeva opposes an order of sanctions.

It is clear Alexeeva has not cooperated with the moving parties' attempts to conduct her deposition. Each time a date was agreed upon, counsel for moving parties served a notice of deposition that provided for the in-person deposition of Alexeeva. Each time these notices of deposition were served, Alexeeva's counsel offered no objection to the terms set forth in the notice. Several of the purported deposition dates were blown up at the last minute by Alexeeva's counsel for reasons never satisfactorily explained. Finally, although Alexeeva's counsel agreed to the last noticed deposition date, he refused to offer Alexeeva for deposition in person. Instead, he insisted that since earlier proceedings in the case had been conducted remotely, Alexeeva's deposition had to proceed via Zoom.

Then counsel for the moving parties filed the pending motion. At the June 27, 2024 hearing before the pro tem, Alexeeva's counsel maintained an in-person deposition should not go forward, because all other proceedings had been remote.

The several deposition notices served by the moving parties each provided for an in-person deposition. Ms. Alexeeva's counsel never objected in any way. Instead, he simply did not produce his client, leading to expenses and further waste of time as the parties conducted more meet and confer efforts to set new deposition dates.

Plaintiff's willingness to appear remotely for deposition does not satisfy her obligation to appear in response to valid notices to which no code-compliant objections were made. It doesn't matter that other proceedings have been conducted remotely in this action. Remote appearances may be convenient but they are not required.

The motion for an order compelling the in-person deposition of Ms. Alexeeva is GRANTED. Her refusals to appear to date are without substantial justification, and therefore sanctions are awarded to the moving parties (but not Mr. Su) in the amount of $2,698.25, payable within 10 days of the filing date of this order. Ms. Alexeeva must appear in person for her deposition on a date to be agreed on by counsel but not later than July 25, 2024. The request for other sanctions such as terminating sanctions is denied.

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare and submit a proposed order in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Curtis E.A. Karnow; Clerk: S. Kane; Not Reported. (302/CK)

Terence O. Mayo; Mayo & Mayo; 415-397-1515; appearing as counsel for plaintiff and cross-defendant Ying-Tsun Su.

Not argued. The court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANTS STATE FARM GENERAL INSURANCE COMPANY AND WENDY PHILLIPS' MOTION TO STRIKE AMENDED COMPLAINT is DENIED. Moving party failed to cite any authority for the proposition that a defendant that has not formally appeared in the action is entitled to notice or is required to be a party to any stipulations filed in the matter. The Court notes that there was no reason for plaintiff's counsel to not reach out to the known counsel for the defendant prior to seeking leave to file an amended complaint.
